meaning_en: The skill or power to do something
example: She has the ability to speak three languages.
mnemonic: ABILITY — ABLE + ITY; being ABLE gives you ability.

meaning_en: Having the skill or means to do something
example: Are you able to finish the project by Friday?
mnemonic: ABLE — a TABLE is ABLE to hold things up; stable and capable.

meaning_en: In or to a foreign country
example: She went abroad to study medicine in Germany.
mnemonic: ABROAD — A + BROAD road leads you out of the country.

meaning_en: To agree to take or receive something
example: She was happy to accept the job offer.
mnemonic: ACCEPT — ACCEss + PT; you get access when someone accepts you.

meaning_en: An unexpected event that causes damage or injury
example: There was a road accident near the school gate.
mnemonic: ACCIDENT — ACCIDENTally happens; no one plans it.

meaning_en: To successfully reach a goal through effort
example: With hard work, you can achieve anything you want.
mnemonic: ACHIEVE — A CHIEF achieves great things for his people.

meaning_en: Doing things regularly; full of energy
example: She stays active by jogging every morning.
mnemonic: ACTIVE — ACT + IVE; always ACTing and doing things.

meaning_en: In fact; used to correct or emphasise something
example: I thought he was angry, but he was actually joking.
mnemonic: ACTUALLY — ACTUAL + LY; what is ACTUAL is what actually happened.

meaning_en: A condition that puts you in a better position
example: Speaking two languages is a big advantage in business.
mnemonic: ADVANTAGE — ADVANCE + AGE; advancing with age gives you advantages.

meaning_en: An exciting and sometimes risky experience
example: Trekking in the Himalayas was a real adventure.
mnemonic: ADVENTURE — VENTURE into the unknown; an ad-venture is a bold venture.

meaning_en: To tell the public about a product to encourage sales
example: Companies advertise their products on social media.
mnemonic: ADVERTISE — ADVERT + ISE; an advert is what you make when you advertise.

meaning_en: Suggestions about what someone should do
example: My teacher gave me good advice about studying.
mnemonic: ADVICE — a DEVICE helps you; advice is like a mental device.

meaning_en: To have an influence on something or someone
example: Pollution affects the health of people living in cities.
mnemonic: AFFECT — A + FECT; infect and affect both change things.

meaning_en: To have the same opinion; to say yes
example: We all agreed that the plan was a good idea.
mnemonic: AGREE — A + GREE; say A-OK and GREE-t the idea warmly.

meaning_en: Living; not dead
example: The plant is still alive even after weeks without water.
mnemonic: ALIVE — A + LIVE; to be alive is to LIVE.

meaning_en: To give permission for something to happen
example: Students are not allowed to use phones in class.
mnemonic: ALLOW — ALL + OW; allow ALL in, no restrictions.

meaning_en: Very nearly but not completely
example: I almost missed the train this morning.
mnemonic: ALMOST — ALL + MOST; almost ALL but not quite MOST.

meaning_en: Without other people; by yourself
example: She lives alone in a small flat near the college.
mnemonic: ALONE — ALL + ONE; when you're alone, you're all one by yourself.

meaning_en: Before now or before a particular time
example: By the time I arrived, she had already left.
mnemonic: ALREADY — ALL + READY; everything is all ready; it's already done.

meaning_en: Despite the fact that; even though
example: Although it was raining, we went for a walk.
mnemonic: ALTHOUGH — ALL + THOUGH; even though ALL signs say otherwise.

meaning_en: A quantity of something
example: A large amount of rain fell last night.
mnemonic: AMOUNT — A MOUNTAIN of it; a huge amount is like a mountain.

meaning_en: Very old; from a long time in the past
example: We visited an ancient temple built 2000 years ago.
mnemonic: ANCIENT — A + NCIENT; AN ANCIENT ruin is very, very old.

meaning_en: To come into sight; to seem
example: A rainbow appeared after the rain stopped.
mnemonic: APPEAR — A + PEER; when you PEER at something, it appears.

meaning_en: To make a formal request; to put something to use
example: She decided to apply for a job at the new company.
mnemonic: APPLY — APPLy yourself; like applying an apple of effort.

meaning_en: To disagree strongly and often loudly
example: The two brothers always argue about what to watch on TV.
mnemonic: ARGUE — A + RGUE; a ROUGE argument turns faces red.

meaning_en: To plan or organise something in advance
example: Can you arrange a meeting for next Monday?
mnemonic: ARRANGE — A RANGE of things put in order = arranged.

meaning_en: To be present at an event or place
example: All students must attend the assembly on Monday.
mnemonic: ATTEND — AT + END; you attend from start to end.

meaning_en: To cause interest or liking; to pull towards
example: The colourful display attracted many visitors to the shop.
mnemonic: ATTRACT — A TRACTOR pulls things; attract pulls interest.

meaning_en: The group of people watching or listening to a performance
example: The audience clapped loudly at the end of the concert.
mnemonic: AUDIENCE — AUDIO + ENCE; the audience hears the audio.

meaning_en: To stay away from something or stop it from happening
example: Try to avoid eating too much junk food.
mnemonic: AVOID — A + VOID; create a void — empty space — between you and the thing.

meaning_en: Very bad or unpleasant
example: The weather was awful — cold, wet and windy all day.
mnemonic: AWFUL — AWE + FUL; full of negative awe; shockingly bad.

meaning_en: To defeat someone in a competition or game
example: Our school team beat the other school 3–0.
mnemonic: BEAT — a DRUM BEAT; rhythm wins the competition.

meaning_en: To act in a particular way; to act politely
example: Please behave well when we visit the guests.
mnemonic: BEHAVE — BE + HAVE; to HAVE good manners means to behave.

meaning_en: To be in the right place or owned by someone
example: This bag belongs to the girl sitting in the front row.
mnemonic: BELONG — BE + LONG; you LONG to belong somewhere.

meaning_en: An advantage or something that helps you
example: Exercise has many health benefits.
mnemonic: BENEFIT — BENE means good in Latin; benefit = good result.

meaning_en: One thousand million (1,000,000,000)
example: There are over a billion people in India.
mnemonic: BILLION — MILL + ION; a billion is way beyond a million.

meaning_en: The red liquid that flows through your body
example: The doctor took a blood sample for testing.
mnemonic: BLOOD — FLOOD; blood floods through your veins like a river.

meaning_en: To take something temporarily with the intention of returning it
example: Can I borrow your pen for a minute?
mnemonic: BORROW — SORROW; you feel sorrow if you forget to return what you borrow.

meaning_en: The organ inside your head that controls thinking
example: Regular reading helps keep your brain sharp.
mnemonic: BRAIN — TRAIN your BRAIN; the brain is like a train that needs track.

meaning_en: Giving out a strong light; or very intelligent
example: She is a bright student who always gets top marks.
mnemonic: BRIGHT — NIGHT becomes bright with stars; bright overcomes dark.

meaning_en: To be on fire or to destroy something with fire
example: The candle will burn for about four hours.
mnemonic: BURN — a TURN near fire and you BURN.

meaning_en: A place where people stay in tents; to stay outdoors in tents
example: We camped by the river for two nights.
mnemonic: CAMP — a CHAMP camps outdoors and survives nature.

meaning_en: Giving attention to avoid mistakes or danger
example: Be careful when crossing a busy road.
mnemonic: CAREFUL — CARE + FUL; full of care = careful.

meaning_en: To grab something moving through the air; to get an illness
example: Can you catch the ball if I throw it?
mnemonic: CATCH — MATCH; you catch the ball in a cricket match.

meaning_en: The reason something happens; to make something happen
example: Smoking can cause serious health problems.
mnemonic: CAUSE — because ends in -cause; every because has a CAUSE.

meaning_en: To do something enjoyable on a special occasion
example: We celebrate Diwali every year with family.
mnemonic: CELEBRATE — CELEB + RATE; celebrities celebrate on a great rate!

meaning_en: Sure; without any doubt
example: I'm certain I left my keys on the table.
mnemonic: CERTAIN — a CURTAIN is pulled open and you see clearly — certain.

meaning_en: An opportunity; or the possibility of something happening
example: This is your chance to prove yourself.
mnemonic: CHANCE — DANCE; take a chance and DANCE with opportunity.

meaning_en: The qualities that make someone who they are; a person in a story
example: The main character in the novel is a brave young girl.
mnemonic: CHARACTER — CHAR + ACTOR; a char(coal) sketch of an actor = character.

meaning_en: An organisation that helps people in need
example: She donates to a children's charity every month.
mnemonic: CHARITY — CHARE + ITY; share (chare) kindness = charity.

meaning_en: Quick to learn and understand; intelligent
example: She is clever enough to solve complex maths problems.
mnemonic: CLEVER — LEVER; a clever person uses a lever (brain) to lift problems.

meaning_en: The typical weather conditions in an area
example: India has a tropical climate with hot summers.
mnemonic: CLIMATE — CLIMB + ATE; the temperature you CLIMB to = climate.

meaning_en: To gather things together over time
example: He collects old coins as a hobby.
mnemonic: COLLECT — COLLECT stamps, toys, memories — all together.

meaning_en: Feeling relaxed and at ease; pleasant to use
example: This sofa is very comfortable to sit on.
mnemonic: COMFORTABLE — COMFORT + ABLE; able to give comfort.

meaning_en: To share information or feelings with others
example: Good teams communicate clearly with each other.
mnemonic: COMMUNICATE — COMMON + UNITY + ATE; sharing creates community.

meaning_en: A group of people living or working together
example: The local community came together to clean the park.
mnemonic: COMMUNITY — COMMON + UNITY; unity among common people = community.

meaning_en: To examine similarities and differences between things
example: Compare the prices before you decide which to buy.
mnemonic: COMPARE — COM + PAIR; put a PAIR of things side by side to compare.

meaning_en: To try to win against others
example: She will compete in the national swimming championship.
mnemonic: COMPETE — COM + PETE; two Peters (Pete) competing against each other.

meaning_en: A contest where people try to win
example: He entered a painting competition and won first prize.
mnemonic: COMPETITION — COMPETE + ITION; what happens when people compete.

meaning_en: To express dissatisfaction about something
example: Customers complained about the slow service.
mnemonic: COMPLAIN — PLAIN food makes you complain; no flavour!

meaning_en: The state something is in; a requirement
example: The car is in excellent condition despite being ten years old.
mnemonic: CONDITION — CONDI + TION; the condition of your hair needs conditioner!

meaning_en: To join two or more things together
example: Connect the cable to the laptop to charge it.
mnemonic: CONNECT — CON + NECT; a NECK connects head and body.

meaning_en: To think carefully about something before deciding
example: Please consider all your options before deciding.
mnemonic: CONSIDER — CON + SIDE + R; look at both sides before you consider.

meaning_en: To keep doing something without stopping
example: Please continue reading from where you stopped.
mnemonic: CONTINUE — CON + TINUE; the TUNE continues playing without break.

meaning_en: To have power over something; to manage it
example: The pilot lost control of the aircraft briefly.
mnemonic: CONTROL — CON + TROLL; a troll who controls the bridge = in control.

meaning_en: An informal talk between two or more people
example: We had a long conversation about our future plans.
mnemonic: CONVERSATION — CON + VERSE + ATION; exchanging verses of talk.

meaning_en: Very foolish or extreme; enthusiastic about something
example: She is crazy about classical music.
mnemonic: CRAZY — HAZY mind; a hazy head leads to crazy thoughts.

meaning_en: Having the ability to make new and original things
example: She is very creative — she designs her own clothes.
mnemonic: CREATIVE — CREATE + IVE; someone who can create anything.

meaning_en: An illegal action that can be punished by law
example: Stealing is a crime that can lead to jail.
mnemonic: CRIME — GRIME; crime leaves a dirty grime on society.

meaning_en: A large group of people gathered together
example: A huge crowd gathered to watch the parade.
mnemonic: CROWD — a LOUD crowd is always noisy.

meaning_en: A situation that may cause harm or injury
example: The hikers were warned about the danger of flash floods.
mnemonic: DANGER — RANGER faces danger in the wild.

meaning_en: No longer alive
example: The plant was dead because no one watered it.
mnemonic: DEAD — DEADly silence; no movement, no sound.

meaning_en: The end of life
example: The death of the old tree saddened the whole village.
mnemonic: DEATH — BREATH stops at DEATH.

meaning_en: A choice made after thinking about something
example: Making the right decision takes time and thought.
mnemonic: DECISION — DECIDE + SION; a decision comes from deciding.

meaning_en: Going far down or inward; intense
example: The well is very deep — almost 50 feet.
mnemonic: DEEP — SLEEP; deep sleep takes you far down.

meaning_en: Without doubt; certainly
example: I will definitely be there on time.
mnemonic: DEFINITELY — DEFINITE + LY; definite = sure = definitely.

meaning_en: To rely on someone or something
example: Success depends on how hard you work.
mnemonic: DEPEND — DE + PEND; a pendulum depends on its string.

meaning_en: To say or write what something is like
example: Can you describe what the man looked like?
mnemonic: DESCRIBE — DE + SCRIBE; a scribe writes descriptions.

meaning_en: A plan or drawing; to create the look of something
example: She designed a logo for the new company.
mnemonic: DESIGN — DE + SIGN; creating a sign or symbol is design.

meaning_en: To damage something so badly it cannot be used
example: The storm destroyed several homes in the village.
mnemonic: DESTROY — DE + STROY; stories (Troy) were destroyed in the war.

meaning_en: To grow or cause something to grow or improve
example: We need to develop new skills for the future.
mnemonic: DEVELOP — DE + VELOP; like an envelope opening and revealing growth.

meaning_en: The way in which things are not the same
example: What is the difference between a virus and a bacteria?
mnemonic: DIFFERENCE — DIFFER + ENCE; when things differ, there's a difference.

meaning_en: Related to computers, the internet, or electronic devices
example: Digital photography has replaced film cameras.
mnemonic: DIGITAL — DIGIT + AL; digits (numbers) power the digital world.

meaning_en: To have a different opinion from someone
example: They disagreed about which film to watch.
mnemonic: DISAGREE — DIS + AGREE; the opposite of agree.

meaning_en: To stop being visible; to go out of sight
example: The magician made the coin disappear.
mnemonic: DISAPPEAR — DIS + APPEAR; the opposite of appearing.

meaning_en: A sudden event causing great damage or suffering
example: The earthquake was the worst disaster in the region.
mnemonic: DISASTER — DIS + ASTER (star); when your lucky star falls, disaster strikes.

meaning_en: To find or learn something for the first time
example: Scientists discovered a new species of frog.
mnemonic: DISCOVER — DIS + COVER; remove the cover and discover what's underneath.

meaning_en: An illness that affects a person, animal, or plant
example: Malaria is a disease caused by mosquito bites.
mnemonic: DISEASE — DIS + EASE; when you're not at ease, you have a disease.

meaning_en: To copy data from the internet to your device
example: You can download the app for free.
mnemonic: DOWNLOAD — DOWN + LOAD; loading data DOWN to your device.

meaning_en: Images or thoughts during sleep; a strong hope for the future
example: Her dream is to become a professional dancer.
mnemonic: DREAM — CREAM; life is a dream as sweet as cream.

meaning_en: To let something fall; to decrease
example: Be careful not to drop the glass.
mnemonic: DROP — a CROP drops to the ground when harvested.

meaning_en: To get money by working
example: She earns a good salary as a software engineer.
mnemonic: EARN — LEARN; you LEARN to EARN money.

meaning_en: The process of teaching and learning
example: Good education opens doors to better opportunities.
mnemonic: EDUCATION — EDUCATE + ION; being educated is the process of education.

meaning_en: Energy used to power lights, machines, and devices
example: The storm cut the electricity supply for two hours.
mnemonic: ELECTRICITY — ELECTRIC + CITY; a city runs on electricity.

meaning_en: Containing nothing; with no one inside
example: The bottle is empty — please get a new one.
mnemonic: EMPTY — EM + PTY; an empty pot says 'pity, nothing inside!'

meaning_en: The ability to be active; power from fuel or electricity
example: Solar panels convert sunlight into energy.
mnemonic: ENERGY — ENTER + GY; energy enters and powers everything.

meaning_en: Extremely large in size or amount
example: An enormous wave crashed onto the beach.
mnemonic: ENORMOUS — E + NORM + OUS; way beyond the normal size.

meaning_en: The natural world around us; surroundings
example: We must protect the environment for future generations.
mnemonic: ENVIRONMENT — ENVIRON + MENT; everything that environs (surrounds) us.

meaning_en: Extremely good; of the highest quality
example: She gave an excellent performance in the final exam.
mnemonic: EXCELLENT — EXCEL + LENT; you EXCEL when you are excellent.

meaning_en: Something that happens to you; knowledge gained from events
example: Living abroad was a wonderful experience.
mnemonic: EXPERIENCE — EXPERT + IENCE; experience makes you an expert.

meaning_en: To make something clear by describing it
example: Can you explain how this machine works?
mnemonic: EXPLAIN — EX + PLAIN; make it PLAIN and simple to understand.

meaning_en: Very great in degree; beyond what is normal
example: Athletes need extreme discipline to reach the top.
mnemonic: EXTREME — EX + TREME; beyond the TRIM, the edge — extreme.

meaning_en: To not succeed at something
example: He failed his driving test the first time but passed the second.
mnemonic: FAIL — FLAIL; you flail around when you fail.

meaning_en: Treating everyone equally; light in colour
example: The teacher was fair and gave everyone the same amount of time.
mnemonic: FAIR — HAIR; fair hair, fair deal — both are balanced.

meaning_en: A popular style of clothing at a particular time
example: She always follows the latest fashion trends.
mnemonic: FASHION — PASSION; when you have a PASSION for clothes, it's fashion.

meaning_en: A feeling of being scared about something dangerous
example: She has a fear of heights and avoids tall buildings.
mnemonic: FEAR — NEAR; fear is near when danger is close.

meaning_en: To use physical force against someone; to argue strongly
example: The two neighbours fought over the boundary wall.
mnemonic: FIGHT — LIGHT; fighters need to be light on their feet.

meaning_en: To concentrate on one thing
example: Focus on your studies during exam time.
mnemonic: FOCUS — a LOCUS point; focus everything at one locus.

meaning_en: From or belonging to another country
example: She loves learning foreign languages.
mnemonic: FOREIGN — FOR + REIGN; a foreign king reigns in another land.

meaning_en: A large area covered with trees
example: Tigers live in deep forests and grasslands.
mnemonic: FOREST — FOR + REST; a forest is where nature rests.

meaning_en: Recently made or produced; not stale or old
example: I love the smell of fresh bread from the bakery.
mnemonic: FRESH — a REFRESH; fresh things refresh you.

meaning_en: Large movable items in a room, like chairs or tables
example: They bought new furniture for their living room.
mnemonic: FURNITURE — FURTHER + NATURE; natural wood goes further as furniture.

meaning_en: A space or opening between two things
example: There was a gap in the fence where the dog got through.
mnemonic: GAP — a MAP has a GAP where roads don't reach.

meaning_en: An aim you want to achieve; a point scored in sport
example: Her goal is to become a doctor by the age of 25.
mnemonic: GOAL — GO + AL; you GO ALL the way to reach your goal.

meaning_en: The group of people who control a country or state
example: The government announced new health policies.
mnemonic: GOVERNMENT — GOVERN + MENT; a mental plan to govern the country.

meaning_en: Something you do regularly without thinking
example: Reading every night is a good habit to develop.
mnemonic: HABIT — RABBIT; a rabbit hops every day — a daily habit.

meaning_en: A pain in the head
example: She got a headache after staring at the screen too long.
mnemonic: HEADACHE — HEAD + ACHE; your HEAD ACHES when you have a headache.

meaning_en: The organ that pumps blood; the centre of emotions
example: Exercise keeps your heart healthy and strong.
mnemonic: HEART — EARTH rearranged; your heart is the earth of your body.

meaning_en: Having great weight; difficult to lift
example: The box was too heavy for me to carry alone.
mnemonic: HEAVY — HEAVE + Y; to heave something means it's heavy.

meaning_en: Someone admired for bravery or great achievements
example: Firefighters are heroes who risk their lives for others.
mnemonic: HERO — ZERO to HERO; going from nothing to greatness.

meaning_en: To put something where it cannot be seen; to conceal yourself
example: She hid the birthday present in the cupboard.
mnemonic: HIDE — a SLIDE hides the playground equipment in winter.

meaning_en: To keep something in your hands or arms
example: Hold on to the railing so you don't fall.
mnemonic: HOLD — GOLD; you HOLD GOLD tightly because it's precious.

meaning_en: Extremely large
example: A huge crowd gathered at the stadium for the final.
mnemonic: HUGE — HUG + E; a huge bear hug covers everything.

meaning_en: A person; relating to people
example: Human beings are the most intelligent creatures on earth.
mnemonic: HUMAN — HUM + AN; humans HUM melodies — only people make music.

meaning_en: To cause physical or emotional pain
example: I fell and hurt my knee on the playground.
mnemonic: HURT — BURST; when something bursts, it hurts.

meaning_en: Unwell; not in good health
example: She was ill with flu for a whole week.
mnemonic: ILL — STILL; when ill, you must stay STILL and rest.

meaning_en: A medical condition; being sick
example: His illness kept him away from school for two weeks.
mnemonic: ILLNESS — ILL + NESS; the state of being ill.

meaning_en: To form a picture in your mind of something
example: Imagine you are on a beach — feel the warm sand.
mnemonic: IMAGINE — IMAGE + INE; creating an image in your mind.

meaning_en: Not able to happen or be done
example: Nothing is impossible if you work hard enough.
mnemonic: IMPOSSIBLE — IM + POSSIBLE; add IM and possible becomes impossible.

meaning_en: To become or make greater in number or amount
example: The price of petrol has increased this month.
mnemonic: INCREASE — IN + CREASE; more creases mean more folds — increasing.

meaning_en: Not needing help from others; free from control
example: She is very independent and manages everything on her own.
mnemonic: INDEPENDENT — IN + DEPEND + ENT; not depending on anyone.

meaning_en: Businesses that produce goods or services
example: The film industry in India is one of the largest in the world.
mnemonic: INDUSTRY — INDUS + TRY; try hard like the Indus civilisation — industrious.

meaning_en: Physical harm caused to the body
example: The footballer suffered a knee injury during the match.
mnemonic: INJURY — IN + JURY; a jury decides about the injury in court.

meaning_en: Having a high ability to learn and understand
example: She is an intelligent student who learns quickly.
mnemonic: INTELLIGENT — INTEL + LIGENT; like Intel chips — fast and smart.

meaning_en: To create something that has never existed before
example: Thomas Edison invented the electric light bulb.
mnemonic: INVENT — IN + VENT; a new VENT (outlet) for a new idea.

meaning_en: To ask someone to come to an event
example: She invited all her friends to her birthday party.
mnemonic: INVITE — IN + VITE; invite brings someone IN to your space.

meaning_en: To push yourself off the ground into the air
example: The kids love to jump on the trampoline.
mnemonic: JUMP — BUMP; you jump and bump up into the air.

meaning_en: To cause the death of something or someone
example: Smoking kills thousands of people every year.
mnemonic: KILL — STILL; what is killed goes still forever.

meaning_en: Information and understanding gained through experience
example: Reading books increases your knowledge.
mnemonic: KNOWLEDGE — KNOW + LEDGE; stand on the ledge of knowing.

meaning_en: A rule made by the government that people must follow
example: Breaking traffic laws can result in a fine.
mnemonic: LAW — JAW; the jaw of justice speaks the law.

meaning_en: Not willing to work or be active
example: Don't be lazy — get up and finish your homework.
mnemonic: LAZY — a HAZY, dazy person is lazy.

meaning_en: To be in charge; to guide others
example: She leads the school debate team.
mnemonic: LEAD — bEAD; the lead bead on a necklace is always out front.

meaning_en: The way a person lives, including habits and activities
example: A healthy lifestyle includes exercise and good food.
mnemonic: LIFESTYLE — LIFE + STYLE; the style in which you live.

meaning_en: Relating to a particular area or neighbourhood
example: We always shop at the local market on Sunday.
mnemonic: LOCAL — LOCO + AL; a local loco (engine) only runs in your area.

meaning_en: Making a lot of noise
example: The music at the party was too loud.
mnemonic: LOUD — a CLOUD with thunder is LOUD.

meaning_en: Having good fortune; fortunate
example: I was lucky to find a seat on the crowded train.
mnemonic: LUCKY — a DUCKY; finding a rubber duck in your pocket — lucky!

meaning_en: Very important or serious; larger in size or number
example: There has been a major change in the company's plan.
mnemonic: MAJOR — MAJOR league; the big, important league.

meaning_en: To be in charge of something; to succeed despite difficulty
example: She manages a team of fifteen people at work.
mnemonic: MANAGE — MAN + AGE; with AGE and experience, a MAN manages well.

meaning_en: To legally become the husband or wife of someone
example: They married in a small ceremony by the beach.
mnemonic: MARRY — MERRY; getting married makes you merry.

meaning_en: Means of communication such as TV, newspapers, and internet
example: Social media has changed how people share news.
mnemonic: MEDIA — MEDIUM; media is the medium that carries messages.

meaning_en: The ability to remember things; something you remember
example: She has a strong memory and never forgets a face.
mnemonic: MEMORY — MEMO + RY; a MEMO is written to help memory.

meaning_en: The part of you that thinks, feels, and remembers
example: A calm mind helps you make better decisions.
mnemonic: MIND — FIND; what you FIND in your head is your MIND.

meaning_en: Not present; lost
example: The police are looking for a missing child.
mnemonic: MISSING — MISS + ING; you MISS something that is missing.

meaning_en: The act of moving; a group working towards a goal
example: The independence movement changed the country's history.
mnemonic: MOVEMENT — MOVE + MENT; the result of moving.

meaning_en: Small in width; limited
example: The road was too narrow for two cars to pass.
mnemonic: NARROW — an ARROW is narrow and pointed.

meaning_en: Existing in or caused by nature; not artificial
example: Honey is a natural sweetener.
mnemonic: NATURAL — NATURE + AL; coming from nature.

meaning_en: Needed; essential for a purpose
example: It is necessary to wear a helmet while riding a bike.
mnemonic: NECESSARY — 1 C and 2 S's; Never Eat Chips, Eat Salad Sandwiches And Remain Young.

meaning_en: Feeling anxious or worried
example: She was very nervous before her first job interview.
mnemonic: NERVOUS — NERVE + OUS; your nerves act up when nervous.

meaning_en: Usual; what you would expect
example: It is normal to feel nervous before an exam.
mnemonic: NORMAL — FORM + AL; the usual form of things is normal.

meaning_en: To become aware of something; an announcement
example: Did you notice the new sign outside the school?
mnemonic: NOTICE — NOTE + ICE; a note that stands out is noticeable.

meaning_en: A very large area of sea
example: The Pacific Ocean is the largest ocean in the world.
mnemonic: OCEAN — O + CEAN; an ocean of dreams — the letter O looks like a wave.

meaning_en: To present something for someone to accept or refuse
example: They offered her the job the very next day.
mnemonic: OFFER — OFF + ER; the price is taken OFF when there's an offer.

meaning_en: Normal; not special or unusual
example: It was an ordinary day — nothing special happened.
mnemonic: ORDINARY — ORDER + INARY; everything in ordinary order.

meaning_en: To plan and arrange something carefully
example: She organised the school trip for 50 students.
mnemonic: ORGANISE — ORGAN + ISE; an organ has many pipes — all organised.

meaning_en: An unpleasant feeling in part of your body
example: She felt a sharp pain in her back after lifting the box.
mnemonic: PAIN — RAIN; pain falls on you like rain.

meaning_en: Specific; more than usual
example: Is there a particular reason you chose this school?
mnemonic: PARTICULAR — PART + ICULAR; a particular PART stands out.

meaning_en: Able to wait calmly; a person receiving medical care
example: You need to be patient — good things take time.
mnemonic: PATIENT — WAIT + PATIENT; a patient patient waits calmly.

meaning_en: A state of calm without war or conflict
example: We must work together to achieve world peace.
mnemonic: PEACE — a PIECE of quiet; one piece of silence = peace.

meaning_en: To do an action or present entertainment publicly
example: She performed a beautiful classical dance at the event.
mnemonic: PERFORM — PER + FORM; through your FORM (body), you perform.

meaning_en: Official or formal agreement that allows something
example: You need permission from the school to go on the trip.
mnemonic: PERMISSION — PERMIT + SION; when you permit someone, you give permission.

meaning_en: Related to the body; real and tangible
example: Physical exercise is important for good health.
mnemonic: PHYSICAL — PHYSIC + AL; physics is the physical world around us.

meaning_en: A large round object that orbits a star
example: Earth is the only planet known to support life.
mnemonic: PLANET — PLANE + T; a planet is a PLANE in T-shape orbit around the sun.

meaning_en: Having good manners; respectful to others
example: Always be polite when speaking to elders.
mnemonic: POLITE — LITE; light, gentle and easy — polite behaviour.

meaning_en: Harmful substances in the environment
example: Air pollution in big cities affects people's health.
mnemonic: POLLUTION — POLLUTE + ION; polluting creates pollution.

meaning_en: Liked or enjoyed by many people
example: Football is the most popular sport in the world.
mnemonic: POPULAR — PEOPLE + POPULAR; what the PEOPLE love is popular.

meaning_en: The ability to do something; electricity or energy
example: Knowledge is power.
mnemonic: POWER — a TOWER generates POWER; stands tall and strong.

meaning_en: To like one thing more than another
example: I prefer tea to coffee in the morning.
mnemonic: PREFER — PRE + FER; you give preference (PREFERence) to one thing.

meaning_en: To make something ready beforehand
example: She prepared well for her board exams.
mnemonic: PREPARE — PRE + PARE; pare (cut) things in advance = prepare.

meaning_en: To stop something from happening
example: Washing your hands can prevent the spread of germs.
mnemonic: PREVENT — PRE + VENT; block the vent before anything gets in.

meaning_en: Relating to a job requiring skill; doing something for payment
example: She is a professional musician who plays at concerts.
mnemonic: PROFESSIONAL — PROFESSION + AL; having a profession makes you professional.

meaning_en: To tell someone you will definitely do something
example: He promised to call her as soon as he arrived.
mnemonic: PROMISE — PRO + MISE; a pro never misses a promise.

meaning_en: To keep someone or something safe from harm
example: Sunscreen protects your skin from UV rays.
mnemonic: PROTECT — PRO + TECT; a pro TECHnician protects your data.

meaning_en: To make books, articles, or content available to the public
example: She published her first novel at the age of 22.
mnemonic: PUBLISH — PUBLIC + ISH; to publish is to make something public-ish.

meaning_en: To move something towards you
example: Pull the door handle to open it.
mnemonic: PULL — BULL; a bull PULLS a heavy cart.

meaning_en: To move something away from you using force
example: Push the trolley to the checkout counter.
mnemonic: PUSH — a BUSH pushed aside as you walk through.

meaning_en: How good something is; a characteristic
example: The quality of this cloth is excellent.
mnemonic: QUALITY — QUAL + ITY; qualifying means reaching a quality level.

meaning_en: A competition to see who is fastest
example: She won the 100-metre race at the school sports day.
mnemonic: RACE — PACE; increase your PACE to win the RACE.

meaning_en: To lift something up; to bring up a child; to increase
example: Please raise your hand if you know the answer.
mnemonic: RAISE — PRAISE; teachers raise and praise students.

meaning_en: To arrive at a place or level; to stretch out to touch something
example: We finally reached the top of the mountain.
mnemonic: REACH — BEACH; you reach the BEACH at the end of your journey.

meaning_en: To become aware of something; to understand suddenly
example: She didn't realise how late it was until the clock struck ten.
mnemonic: REALISE — REAL + ISE; making something REAL in your mind.

meaning_en: To get or accept something given to you
example: She was delighted to receive a gift from her friend.
mnemonic: RECEIVE — RE + CEIVE; CE-IVE sounds like 'seize'; you seize what you receive.

meaning_en: To suggest something as being good or suitable
example: Can you recommend a good restaurant nearby?
mnemonic: RECOMMEND — RE + COMMEND; to commend again is to recommend.

meaning_en: To make something smaller or less
example: We should reduce plastic use to help the environment.
mnemonic: REDUCE — RE + DUCE; duck down (reduce) the amount.

meaning_en: To say you will not do or accept something
example: She refused to answer the question.
mnemonic: REFUSE — RE + FUSE; when a fuse blows, it refuses to work.

meaning_en: The way people or things are connected
example: She has a strong relationship with her parents.
mnemonic: RELATIONSHIP — RELATE + SHIP; a ship that carries two people = relationship.

meaning_en: To take something away from its place
example: Please remove your shoes before entering the temple.
mnemonic: REMOVE — RE + MOVE; move something away = remove it.

meaning_en: To fix something that is broken or damaged
example: The mechanic repaired the car in three hours.
mnemonic: REPAIR — RE + PAIR; pair up the broken pieces again to repair.

meaning_en: To put something new in the place of something old
example: She replaced the broken screen on her phone.
mnemonic: REPLACE — RE + PLACE; place something new in the same spot.

meaning_en: A written or spoken account of something; to tell officially
example: The journalist wrote a report about the flood.
mnemonic: REPORT — RE + PORT; bringing information back to port (place).

meaning_en: Having a duty to deal with something; reliable
example: You are responsible for keeping your desk clean.
mnemonic: RESPONSIBLE — RESPOND + ABLE; able to respond to duties.

meaning_en: Protected from danger or harm
example: Keep your password safe and do not share it.
mnemonic: SAFE — a CAVE is SAFE shelter from storms.

meaning_en: To keep something for future use; to rescue someone
example: She saves a little money every month.
mnemonic: SAVE — a CAVE saves you from rain; shelter = save.

meaning_en: Feeling afraid; frightened
example: The little boy was scared of thunder.
mnemonic: SCARED — SHARED; fear is shared among scared people.

meaning_en: To look carefully for something
example: She searched online for the best hotel deals.
mnemonic: SEARCH — RESEARCH; you re-search when you search again.

meaning_en: One of the four parts of the year: spring, summer, autumn, winter
example: Summer is her favourite season because of the warm weather.
mnemonic: SEASON — SEA + SON; the sea changes with every season.

meaning_en: Something known only to a few people
example: Please keep this a secret — don't tell anyone.
mnemonic: SECRET — SECRET agent hides the truth.

meaning_en: To appear to be; to give the impression of
example: She seems tired — maybe she didn't sleep well.
mnemonic: SEEM — DREAM; things in a dream SEEM real but aren't.

meaning_en: To give something to someone in exchange for money
example: He sells vegetables at the local market every day.
mnemonic: SELL — a BELL rings in the market when you sell.

meaning_en: Important and requiring careful thought; not joking
example: This is a serious matter — we need to act quickly.
mnemonic: SERIOUS — SERIES; a serious series of events.

meaning_en: To divide something among people; to let others use something
example: She shared her lunch with a friend who forgot theirs.
mnemonic: SHARE — CARE; when you CARE, you SHARE.

meaning_en: Almost the same but not exactly the same
example: The two sisters look very similar.
mnemonic: SIMILAR — SIMILE; a simile shows similar things.

meaning_en: Easy to understand or do; not complex
example: The instructions were simple and easy to follow.
mnemonic: SIMPLE — a DIMPLE is a small simple smile.

meaning_en: An ability to do something well through practice
example: Communication is an important skill in any job.
mnemonic: SKILL — FILL; fill yourself with skills.

meaning_en: A happy expression with the corners of your mouth turned up
example: She smiled when she heard the good news.
mnemonic: SMILE — MILE; a smile stretches a MILE across your face.

meaning_en: Relating to society; involving being with other people
example: She is very social and loves meeting new people.
mnemonic: SOCIAL — SOCIETY; social behaviour builds society.

meaning_en: An answer to a problem
example: We need to find a solution to the traffic problem.
mnemonic: SOLUTION — SOLVE + TION; a solution is what you get after you solve.

meaning_en: How fast something or someone moves
example: The car was travelling at a speed of 120 km/h.
mnemonic: SPEED — SEED; a seed grows at its own speed.

meaning_en: A raised platform for performance; a period in development
example: The singer walked onto the stage to perform.
mnemonic: STAGE — a CAGE is the stage for wild animals in a circus.

meaning_en: To take something that belongs to someone else without permission
example: Someone stole his bicycle from outside the station.
mnemonic: STEAL — REAL; what you steal is someone's REAL possession.

meaning_en: Unusual or unexpected; not familiar
example: There was a strange noise coming from the old building.
mnemonic: STRANGE — a STRANGER is always strange at first.

meaning_en: Mental or emotional pressure; to emphasise something
example: Too much work can cause stress and health problems.
mnemonic: STRESS — PRESS; when you're pressed too hard, you feel stress.

meaning_en: To achieve what you wanted to do
example: If you study hard, you will succeed in the exam.
mnemonic: SUCCEED — SUCCESS; you succeed when you reach success.

meaning_en: Having achieved your goals
example: She is a successful businesswoman who started from nothing.
mnemonic: SUCCESSFUL — SUCCESS + FUL; full of success.

meaning_en: To put forward an idea for others to consider
example: Can you suggest a good book to read?
mnemonic: SUGGEST — SUG + GEST; a gesture of suggestion.

meaning_en: To give help or encouragement to someone
example: Her parents always supported her dreams.
mnemonic: SUPPORT — SUP + PORT; a support port holds the structure up.

meaning_en: An unexpected event; to cause a feeling of shock or wonder
example: They gave her a surprise party on her birthday.
mnemonic: SURPRISE — SUR + PRISE; a prize that comes unexpectedly.

meaning_en: Having a taste like sugar; kind and gentle
example: This mango is very sweet and juicy.
mnemonic: SWEET — MEET; it's SWEET to MEET someone you love.

meaning_en: The use of science and knowledge to create tools or machines
example: Modern technology has made life much easier.
mnemonic: TECHNOLOGY — TECHNO + LOGY; the logic of techniques = technology.

meaning_en: How hot or cold something is
example: The temperature dropped to 5°C last night.
mnemonic: TEMPERATURE — TEMPER; a TEMPER rises with TEMPERATURE.

meaning_en: Not wide or thick; having little fat on the body
example: The walls are so thin you can hear everything next door.
mnemonic: THIN — a PIN is THIN; both are narrow.

meaning_en: To send something through the air by moving your arm
example: Throw the ball towards the wicket.
mnemonic: THROW — CROW; a crow throws seeds when planting.

meaning_en: Neat and organised
example: Keep your room tidy and it will feel more pleasant.
mnemonic: TIDY — READY; when tidy, everything is ready and organised.

meaning_en: An instrument used to do a particular job
example: A hammer is a tool used to hit nails into wood.
mnemonic: TOOL — POOL; you need a TOOL to build a POOL.

meaning_en: To feel something with your hand; to make contact
example: Don't touch the wet paint on the wall.
mnemonic: TOUCH — a COUCH; you TOUCH the soft couch with your hand.

meaning_en: A journey to several places; to travel around an area
example: They went on a tour of historical monuments in Delhi.
mnemonic: TOUR — a DETOUR goes around; a tour visits many places.

meaning_en: The business of providing services for tourists
example: Tourism is a major industry in Goa.
mnemonic: TOURISM — TOURIST + ISM; the practice of being a tourist.

meaning_en: A custom or belief passed down over generations
example: Lighting diyas is a tradition during Diwali.
mnemonic: TRADITION — TRADE + ITION; old TRADES and customs = traditions.

meaning_en: A system for carrying people or goods from place to place
example: Public transport in Mumbai includes trains and buses.
mnemonic: TRANSPORT — TRANS + PORT; carry (port) across (trans) = transport.

meaning_en: Problems or difficulties; to worry or bother someone
example: He got into trouble for not submitting his assignment.
mnemonic: TROUBLE — DOUBLE; trouble DOUBLES your stress.

meaning_en: Having all the characteristics you would expect; usual
example: It was a typical rainy day in Mumbai.
mnemonic: TYPICAL — TYPE + ICAL; the typical type of thing.

meaning_en: Not happy; sad or displeased
example: She was unhappy with the result of her exam.
mnemonic: UNHAPPY — UN + HAPPY; not happy at all.

meaning_en: Not common; different from what is normal
example: It is unusual to see snow in Chennai.
mnemonic: UNUSUAL — UN + USUAL; not the usual thing.

meaning_en: Unhappy, worried, or angry about something
example: He was upset when he found out he didn't get the job.
mnemonic: UPSET — UP + SET; when things are set upside down, you're upset.

meaning_en: Able to help you do something; practical
example: A good dictionary is a very useful tool for learners.
mnemonic: USEFUL — USE + FUL; full of use = useful.

meaning_en: A low area of land between hills or mountains
example: The Kullu Valley is famous for its scenic beauty.
mnemonic: VALLEY — a VALLEY is where you FALL Y (way) down between mountains.

meaning_en: Different types of the same thing; a range of choices
example: The market offers a wide variety of fresh vegetables.
mnemonic: VARIETY — VARY + IETY; many things that VARY = variety.

meaning_en: What you can see from a place; an opinion
example: The view from the hilltop was breathtaking.
mnemonic: VIEW — a WINDOW has a VIEW; look through and see.

meaning_en: The sound you make when speaking or singing
example: She has a beautiful voice and sings in the school choir.
mnemonic: VOICE — CHOICE; use your VOICE to make your CHOICE heard.

meaning_en: Armed conflict between countries or groups
example: The war caused great destruction and suffering.
mnemonic: WAR — SCAR; war leaves scars on nations.

meaning_en: Not strong; lacking power or health
example: She felt weak after being ill for several days.
mnemonic: WEAK — a LEAK makes a structure weak.

meaning_en: A ceremony where two people get married
example: We attended a beautiful wedding in Jaipur last month.
mnemonic: WEDDING — WED + DING; the WED(ding) ring goes DING as it's put on.

meaning_en: Living in nature; not controlled; very excited or extreme
example: Tigers are wild animals that live in forests.
mnemonic: WILD — a CHILD in the WILD; uncontrollable and free.

meaning_en: To be the best or first in a competition
example: Our team worked hard to win the championship.
mnemonic: WIN — a GRIN; you GRIN when you WIN.

meaning_en: A desire for something to happen; to hope for something
example: I wish you all the best for your future.
mnemonic: WISH — a FISH in a pond grants WISHES in fairy tales.

meaning_en: Feeling anxious about something that might happen
example: She was worried about the results of her medical test.
mnemonic: WORRIED — HURRIED; when worried, your mind is hurried with thoughts.

meaning_en: To feel anxious about something; a feeling of concern
example: Don't worry — everything will be fine.
mnemonic: WORRY — a STORY that goes wrong makes you worry.
